rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON NOVOTNEY VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON NOVOTNEY VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Imo Industries Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence and strict liability causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to present evidence negating an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Even if the entirety of Mr. Salzmann's declaration is considered, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all facts and evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA BROUSSARD, et al VS. B.R. FUNSTEN & COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mrs. Broussard was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Brenda Broussard's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to The Skynoloid Co.'s General Order No. 129 interrogatory responses are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA BROUSSARD, et al VS. B.R. FUNSTEN & COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Chevron U.S.A., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted in part and denied in part and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as moot as to issue 1 (premises liability) and denied as to issue 2 (conspiracy). As to Mrs. Broussard's exposure to asbestos attributable to Chevron's roofing product, summary judgment is denied. Dr. Horn's declaration creates a triable issue whether exposure to such product was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mrs. Broussard's risk of developing mesothelioma. As to Mrs. Broussard's alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to work performed by or around her husband during his employment at the Standard Oil Building, partial summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mrs. Broussard was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant related to her husband's work at the Standard Oil Building. Therefore plaintiffs' causes of action based on premises liability (the sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, tenth, sixteenth, seventeenth, eighteenth, nineteenth and twentieth causes of action) are dismissed. Summary adjudication of issue 1 (the premises liability causes of action pursuant to Campbell v. Ford Motor Co.) is off calendar as moot pursuant to the above ruling dismissing the premises liability causes of action. Summary adjudication of issue 2 (fifth and fifteenth causes of action for conspiracy) is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support their conspiracy causes of action. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery designed to elicit all facts and evidence plaintiffs have to support their claims for conspiracy, and by failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Mannington Mills, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mrs. Broussard was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' failure to attach the cited standard interrogatory responses (plaintiffs' Exhibit D) are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
